sprint: Cloud-Primary Project Storage — Phase 2: World Builder rewire
task: everything should be on cloud... world & studio both. i dont want any kind of trouble later on. no data loss beacause of whatsoever reason. This phase's own root-app change is a small, proactive, still-uncalled hardening of js/creatorProjectCache.js (Studio's own Phase 4 target, unwired until then): _persistOne()'s catch branch now falls back to the same emergency localStorage write World Builder's own cache module already had, and putLocal() gained the matching opts.onPersistFailed callback -- both purely additive, zero behaviour-change risk. The real Phase 2 work -- rewiring tools/world-builder-v2/js/projectStore.js to route through js/projectCache.js's in-memory mirror, retiring _attemptQuotaSelfHeal, adding ProjectStore.onPersistError()/ProjectCache.onSyncStateChange()/ProjectCache.forceSync() -- is confined to tools/world-builder-v2/. See CLAUDE.md's own entry for the full design and verification.
